content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only .. 10. Разделение ответственности
Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DIRECTION title=Memory updates must use retrieval and reconciliation, not append-only writes summary=The architecture requires a four-step write flow: decide whether to write, retrieve similar/conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplication, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only' entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=append-only,retrieval-before-update,reconciliation,write policy,supersede tags=memory_architecture,write_path,policy evidence=Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Responsibility split: runtime vs memory manager vs storage layer summary=`User-facing runtime` handles dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation, while heavy reconciliation and repair are excluded from runtime. `Memory worker / manager` owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention; `Storage layer` owns immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional update...[truncated 37 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' entities=entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<1>,entity:<7>,entity:<8> keywords=User-facing runtime,Memory worker / manager,Storage layer,separation of responsibilities tags=architecture,component_roles,ownership evidence=### 10.1 User-facing runtime

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended architecture flow: Memory Router → Write-Time Retrieval → Entity Canonicalizer → reconcilers/storage/workers summary=The recommended pipeline routes inputs through Memory Router decisions (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed), then Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized writers/reconcilers (including Note Reconciler and Claim Reconciler) into Postgres + pgvector, with background maintenance workers (Background Consolidator, Memory Repair Worker, Retention / Forgetting Worker) a...[truncated 53 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' keywords=Memory Router,Write-Time Retrieval,Entity Canonicalizer,Note Reconciler,Postgres + pgvector tags=recommended_architecture,pipeline,component_roles useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Architecture v3 storage tiers define five-layer memory roles (Tier A–E) summary=Section '5. Storage tiers' defines a five-tier model: Tier A for small prompt-hot core context, Tier B for typed structured long-term memory, Tier C for rich semantic notes, Tier D for immutable/raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes and filters even if data is in one Postgres. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers' keywords=Storage tiers,Tier A,Tier B,Tier C,Tier D,Tier E tags=memory_architecture,tiering,doc_digest useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim vs Note write criteria in Memory Architecture v3 summary=Use `Claim` for explicit, normalizable, precision-answerable information that can be compared via predicate policy; use `Note` for already-important context-rich or early-stage information useful for future retrieval but not yet a hard fact. When material contains both explicit facts/tasks and rationale/direction, write both. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 7. Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`' keywords=Claim,Note,predicate policy,write criteria tags=memory_architecture,write_path,policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=System goal for Memory Layer: persistent, updatable, explainable, and lifecycle-managed memory summary=The memory layer goal is to persist across sessions, store facts/preferences/tasks/rationale/evolving plans, support knowledge updates, preserve provenance, avoid making every turn permanent, and support aging, archiving, and repair. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы' keywords=memory layer goal,cross-session memory,provenance,knowledge updates,archive and repair tags=goals,requirements,memory_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Structured Scope in v3 requires dual representation with mandatory scope_json envelope summary=Section '6. Structured scope' states that v3 makes scope explicit by storing both human-readable `scope_text` and machine-readable `scope_json`; the minimal `scope_json` fields are `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation predictability and reduce retrieval overgeneralization/conflicts. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 6. Structured scope' keywords=Structured scope,scope_text,scope_json,scope_kind,project_id tags=scope,reconciliation,retrieval_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Architecture v3 defines two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths summary=In Memory Architecture v3, writes are split into Direct structured path (for safely normalizable items like explicit facts, stable preferences, commitments/tasks, and clear status changes) and Note path (for important but not-yet-hard facts such as design direction, rationale/trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons/patterns, document digests, and contextual framing). Maintenance is split into Consolid...[truncated 100 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи' keywords=Direct structured path,Note path,Consolidation,Repair,memory degradation tags=memory_architecture_v3,write_model,maintenance_paths useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory Layer v3 uses four architectural axes to avoid memory failure modes summary=In Memory Architecture v3, the memory layer is designed around four axes—Representation, Lifecycle, Retrieval policy, and Scope/lineage—to prevent devolving into either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' keywords=Memory Architecture v3,four axes,Representation,Lifecycle,Retrieval policy,Scope and lineage tags=architecture,memory_layer,v3,design_principle useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Truth state and retrieval state must remain separate summary=`Truth state` tracks epistemic validity, while `Retrieval state` tracks search/hot-path handling. Claims and Notes have different truth-state sets, and retrieval-state uses a shared lifecycle (`active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`), allowing items to be epistemically outdated yet still retrievable for rationale or historical evidence. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state' keywords=Truth state,Retrieval state,active,warm tags=state_model,retrieval_policy,lifecycle useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
